questions.question: Which element should wrap the primary navigation links for a site?
questions.explanation: <nav> is the semantic landmark for major navigation blocks.

questions.question: Where should the page's main unique content live?
questions.explanation: <main> identifies the dominant content; avoid multiple per page unless hidden.

questions.question: Which element is most appropriate for a self-contained blog post preview?
questions.explanation: <article> represents independent, distributable content.

questions.question: What is the correct way to associate a <label> with an input when they are siblings?
options: Set label for equal to input id
questions.explanation: for/id pairing improves accessibility and click target size.

questions.question: Which input type enforces a numeric range in supporting browsers without JavaScript?
questions.explanation: number inputs honor min, max, and step for native validation.

questions.question: What happens when this form is submitted?
questions.explanation: Forms default to method="get"; without a method attribute the browser issues GET with query parameters.

questions.question: Which attribute makes a field required for native constraint validation?
questions.explanation: required triggers built-in validation on submit.

questions.question: What does autocomplete="off" on a form primarily affect?
options: Browser suggestion of previously entered values
questions.explanation: It hints browsers not to autofill; behavior varies by browser and field type.

questions.question: For grouping related radio buttons, which is correct?
options: Same name on each input in the group
questions.explanation: Radios with the same name are mutually exclusive in a group.

questions.question: Which property aligns flex items along the cross axis in a row flex container?
questions.explanation: align-items controls cross-axis alignment; justify-content is main axis.

questions.question: In a row flex container, which item will grow to fill leftover space?
questions.explanation: flex-grow: 1 on .b allows it to absorb free space; .a has grow 0.

questions.question: What does justify-content: space-between do?
options: Adds equal margin around each item
First at start, last at end, equal space between
questions.explanation: space-between distributes free space between items, not at edges.

questions.question: Which value of flex-direction stacks items vertically with main axis top-to-bottom?
questions.explanation: column sets main axis vertically.

questions.question: Which property defines track sizes for columns?
questions.explanation: grid-template-columns explicitly sizes column tracks.

questions.question:
How many columns does this create?
grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
questions.explanation: repeat(3, 1fr) creates three equal fractional columns.

questions.question: What does grid-area: 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 mean?
options: Row start 1, col start 2, row end 3, col end 4
questions.explanation: grid-area shorthand is row-start / col-start / row-end / col-end.

questions.question: Which unit fr represents in CSS Grid?
options: Fraction of free space in the grid container
questions.explanation: fr distributes remaining space after non-flexible tracks.

questions.question: What does the class `flex items-center justify-between` apply?
options: Flex container, cross-axis center, main-axis space-between
questions.explanation: flex creates flex container; items-center and justify-between are flex utilities.

questions.question: In Tailwind, how do you apply hover styles?
questions.explanation: Variant prefixes like hover: precede utility names.

questions.question: What is the purpose of `@tailwind base` in an input CSS file?
questions.explanation: @tailwind directives pull in Tailwind's layers (base, components, utilities).

questions.question: Which class adds padding on all sides at the `md` breakpoint and above?
questions.explanation: Responsive variants prefix utilities: md:p-4.

questions.question: What is the result of typeof null in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: typeof null is a long-standing quirk returning "object".

questions.question:
What is logged?
console.log([1, 2] + [3, 4]);
questions.explanation: Arrays coerce to strings via toString, producing "1,2" + "3,4".

questions.question: Which statement about `const` is true?
options: The binding cannot be reassigned; object properties may still change
const must be initialized at declaration in all engines
questions.explanation: const prevents rebinding, not mutation of object contents.
var is function-scoped; i remains 3 after the loop.

questions.question: What does Array.prototype.map return?
options: A new array with transformed elements
questions.explanation: map always returns a new array of the same length.

questions.question: Which compares two values without type coercion?
questions.explanation: Strict equality === does not coerce types.
f closes over the outer binding and assigns 2.

questions.question: What does the spread operator ...obj do when obj is a plain object in an object literal?
options: Copies enumerable own properties into the new object
questions.explanation: Object spread copies enumerable own properties (shallow).

questions.question: What is the main purpose of TypeScript?
options: Add static types checked at compile time
questions.explanation: TypeScript is a typed superset that compiles to JavaScript.

questions.question:
What error does TypeScript report?
let x: number = "5";
options: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'
questions.explanation: Explicit annotation number rejects string literal.

questions.question: What does the `interface` keyword create?
options: A structural type shape for compile-time checking
questions.explanation: Interfaces describe object shapes and are erased at compile time.

questions.question: How is `type Alias = { id: string }` different from an equivalent interface?
options: type can express unions and primitives; interface is mainly for object shapes
questions.explanation: type aliases are more flexible for unions/intersections; interfaces support declaration merging.

questions.question:
What is the inferred type of `t`?
const t = [1, "a"] as const;
questions.explanation: as const makes the tuple readonly with literal types.

questions.question: What does `?` on a property mean in an interface?
questions.explanation: Optional properties may be omitted or explicitly undefined.

questions.question: Which utility type makes all properties readonly?
questions.explanation: Readonly<T> maps properties to readonly variants.

questions.question: What is `never` used for?
options: Values that should never occur; exhaustiveness checks
questions.explanation: never represents impossible types and helps exhaustive switch checking.

questions.question: Which attribute provides accessible name when visible label text is insufficient?
questions.explanation: ARIA naming attributes expose accessible names to assistive tech.

questions.question: What does flex-wrap: wrap enable?
options: Items move to additional flex lines when they overflow
questions.explanation: wrap allows multi-line flex layouts.

questions.question: Which property gaps both rows and columns in modern CSS Grid?
questions.explanation: gap is the standard shorthand; grid-gap is legacy alias.

questions.question: What does optional chaining `obj?.prop` return if obj is null?
questions.explanation: Optional chaining short-circuits to undefined when base is nullish.

questions.question: What does `strictNullChecks` enable?
options: null and undefined are distinct types not assignable to other types without narrowing
questions.explanation: With strictNullChecks, null/undefined must be handled explicitly.
